papers.paper_scope: Analysis of channel segregates formation in castings, emphasizing the role of defects in the dendrite network and a criterion for occurrence.

unmapped_findings: The growth of channel segregates is greatly assisted by the presence of small defects in the dendrite network.
The condition [10] should provide a more reliable indication of the occurrence of channel segregates than [1].

Channel segregates may form in situations where the interdendritic flow direction is substantially parallel to isotherms, because defects oriented out of the plane of the isotherms can divert interdendritic flow to a direction which will lead to channel segregation.
The approximations made in the derivation of [10] make it only semiquantitatively accurate.
